Component.Name: Mission Control State Machine
Component.Purpose: Central mission coordinator that owns the top-level mission state machine (defined formally in FSM--1) and orchestrates exploration, return-to-start, and second run behaviors. Responsibilities: - Maintain mission state with states {IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, COMPLETED}, with behavior and transitions specified in FSM--1. - Consume initialization, exploration-status, planning, and second-run status indications. - Periodically trigger frontier target selection, path planning, and motion execution based on current state. - Enforce exploration start/stop conditions, including sensor-availability and time-limit conditions. - Invoke return-to-start and second-run services and relay their status to external reporting. Determinism of transitions (for a given state and input set per control-loop cycle) is guaranteed by FSM--1.

Signal.Name: MC_FrontierRequest
Signal.Description: Request sent in EXPLORE to frontier-generation module to compute or refresh the current set of frontier cells. Contains: current robot cell index (row, col), current mission time, and remaining exploration time (ms). Triggered once per exploration control cycle where target reselection is required.

Signal.Name: MC_FrontierTargetSelectionContext
Signal.Description: Optional context information for frontier target selection, including: - Current cell index. - Current goal region, if known. - Nominal cell traversal duration. Intended to allow frontier module or associated heuristic to compute expected information gain and feasibility.

Signal.Name: MC_PathPlanningRequest
Signal.Description: Request to path planner to compute a path for: - Exploration navigation to a selected frontier target cell (EXPLORE state). - Return-to-start path (RETURN_TO_START state). - Second-run fastest path (SECOND_RUN state). Fields: - RequestID. - Mode: {EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN}. - StartCell (row, col). - GoalSpec: single goal cell or goal region set. - TraversalCostProfileID (points to cost parameters for exploration or second-run).

Signal.Name: MC_MotionCommand
Signal.Description: High-level motion command to motion control / motion primitive module. Encodes: - Path to execute (list of cell centers) or next motion primitive. - Execution mode: {ExplorationSpeed, ReturnSpeed, SecondRunSpeed}. - Safety constraints: min clearance M, emergency stop thresholds. Generated each control loop when in EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, or SECOND_RUN and a valid path exists.

Signal.Name: MC_SecondRunStatusOutput
Signal.Description: Enumerated status output for external reporting: Values: {SECOND_RUN_NOT_EXECUTED, SUCCESS, FAILURE, ABORTED_TIMEOUT, ABORTED_COLLISION}. Updated when entering SECOND_RUN, on completion, or on abort.

Signal.Name: FGM_FrontierSet
Signal.Description: Set of frontier cells computed by frontier-generation module. Structure: list of (row, col) cells that are UNKNOWN and 4-neighbors of FREE cells. Includes timestamp and map revision number to detect stale data.

Signal.Name: PL_PathPlanningResponse
Signal.Description: Response from path planner: - RequestID echo. - Status: {SUCCESS, FAILURE_NO_PATH, FAILURE_TIMEOUT, INVALID_GOAL}. - Path: ordered list of cell indices. - EstimatedTravelCost and EstimatedTravelTime. Used by mission control to command motion or handle planning failure.

Signal.Name: INIT_COMPLETED
Signal.Description: Flag or event from initialization/scheduler module indicating successful completion of system initialization. Sampled each control loop while in IDLE. Mission control uses this along with valid sensor data to transition to EXPLORE.

Signal.Name: ExplorationEvents
Signal.Description: Events from exploration-status module: - EXPLORATION_COMPLETED: map completeness achieved. - EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT: global mapping time limit reached. These events are enqueued into a system event queue; mission control drains the queue once per control loop and updates state accordingly.

Signal.Name: ReturnToStartStatus
Signal.Description: Status from return-to-start service/motion execution: - {RUNNING, REACHED_START, PLANNING_FAILURE, ABORTED_COLLISION}. Mission control uses this to determine when to initiate SECOND_RUN or declare failure.

Signal.Name: SecondRunExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: Status from second-run execution logic: - {RUNNING, SUCCESS_REACHED_GOAL, FAILURE_COLLISION, FAILURE_NO_PATH}. Mission control propagates this into COMPLETED state and MC_SecondRunStatusOutput.

Signal.Name: SensorAvailabilityStatus
Signal.Description: Boolean from sensor abstraction layer indicating that at least one valid set of ultrasonic and infrared measurements has been acquired in current mission. Used along with INIT_COMPLETED to trigger transition from IDLE to EXPLORE.

Signal.Name: PoseEstimate
Signal.Description: Current discrete pose estimate: (row, col, headingIndex). Used in EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN to determine start cell for path planning and to detect arrival at target cells or goal region cells.

Signal.Name: GoalRegionInfo
Signal.Description: Selected 2x2 goal region for second run: - Set of 1–4 FREE goal cells (row, col). - Region ID and metadata. Provided upon exploration completion and prior to SECOND_RUN state planning.

Signal.Name: MC_StateBroadcast
Signal.Description: Broadcast of current mission state for logging or visualization: {IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, COMPLETED}. Emitted each control loop or on state changes.

Signal.Name: Sched_Timebase
Signal.Description: Control loop tick and elapsed mission time from scheduler. Includes: - Loop index. - ElapsedTimeMs since exploration start. Used internally to enforce timing behavior and exploration time limits.

Component.Execution: Executed once per iteration of the periodic control loop (CMP--10), after sensor acquisition and map update, before motion output. Conditions: - System is running (not in hardware-level fault). - INIT_COMPLETED flag available for IDLE->EXPLORE. - Uses exploration events, planner responses, and motion status to drive deterministic transitions between mission states as defined by FSM--1. The behavior of this component, including all state transitions and their guards, is fully specified by state machine FSM--1 in the StateMachines artifact.
Component.Timing: - Invoked exactly once per control loop with period T defined by scheduler (LL--SCHED--1.1.1). - EXPLORE behavior: frontier requests and planning requests are limited to at most one per control loop but may be throttled (e.g., only when previous path has been consumed or invalidated). - RETURN_TO_START and SECOND_RUN: planning invoked once per entry; replanning invoked when map invalidations arise. - All decisions must complete within the control loop budget; mission control logic is O(1) to O(N_frontiers) per cycle, where N_frontiers is bounded by 32 cells in a 4x8 grid.
Component.Invariants: - Mission state is always one of {IDLE, EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN, COMPLETED}. - State transitions are deterministic for a given history of events and time; in each control-loop cycle, for any combination of evaluated inputs/events, at most one outgoing transition from the current state is taken, with fixed priority rules when multiple triggers are simultaneously true. - While in COMPLETED, no new motion commands are issued to motion control. - While in IDLE, motors remain stopped except for test routines external to this component. - Only one of EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, or SECOND_RUN can be active at a time.
Component.Failures: - Planning failure in RETURN_TO_START: Trigger: PL_PathPlanningResponse with FAILURE_NO_PATH or FAILURE_TIMEOUT for return-to-start request. Effect: Mission control records return failure, may either: * Transition directly to COMPLETED with MC_SecondRunStatusOutput=FAILURE, or * Remain in RETURN_TO_START and request retry with backoff (implementation policy encoded via AdditionalData). - Planning failure in SECOND_RUN: Trigger: PL_PathPlanningResponse with FAILURE_NO_PATH or FAILURE_TIMEOUT for second-run request. Effect: MC_SecondRunStatusOutput=FAILURE; transition to COMPLETED. - Exploration timeout: Trigger: EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event prior to EXPLORATION_COMPLETED. Effect: EXPLORE->RETURN_TO_START, with cause logged as TIMEOUT; second run still attempted if return succeeds. - Unexpected event in COMPLETED: Any further exploration or planning events are ignored; MC_SecondRunStatusOutput remains latched.

Component.Name: Frontier Generation and Exploration Target Selection
Component.Purpose: Provides frontier-based exploration capabilities: - Maintains and computes frontier cells based on current grid map. - Offers functions to retrieve current frontier set. - Implements heuristic to prioritize frontier targets using estimated travel-time and expected information gain. - Enforces time-aware filtering of candidate targets based on remaining exploration time and nominal cell traversal duration.

Signal.Name: FGM_FrontierSet
Signal.Description: Set of frontier cells: UNKNOWN cells that are 4-neighbors of at least one FREE cell. Represented as vector of (row, col). Recomputed: - On demand (MC_FrontierRequest). - Whenever a cell transitions from UNKNOWN to FREE/BLOCKED or a wall changes classification, via map-change notifications.

Signal.Name: FGM_SelectedTarget
Signal.Description: Single frontier cell chosen as exploration target based on heuristic. Structure: - TargetCell (row, col). - EstimatedTravelTimeMs. - EstimatedNewlyObservableCells. - CompositeHeuristicValue.

Signal.Name: MC_FrontierRequest
Signal.Description: Trigger from mission control to update frontier set and provide a new frontier target. Includes: current robot cell, remaining exploration time, and cost profile ID.

Signal.Name: GM_MapQuery
Signal.Description: Queries to grid map: - GetCellState(row, col) -> {UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED}. - GetNeighborCells4(row, col) -> list of valid neighbor indices. - GetWallState between cells.

Signal.Name: TimeAwarePlanningInfo
Signal.Description: RemainingTimeMs and NominalCellTraversalDurationMs from time-constraint module. Used to discard frontier candidates whose estimated travel time + mapping overhead exceeds RemainingTimeMs.

Signal.Name: FGM_TargetEstimates
Signal.Description: For each frontier candidate, FGM can optionally expose: - Estimated path cost and time using current path cost model. - Estimated information gain (number of UNKNOWN cells likely to be observed from that cell, constrained by sensor ranges). Returned for debugging or to allow planner to participate in target selection.

Signal.Name: PL_TravelTimeEstimates
Signal.Description: Helper interface from path planner used when FGM requests approximate costs to a set of frontier cells without computing full individual paths (e.g., multi-goal heuristics). Encodes estimated traversal times by reusing path cost metric.

Component.Execution: Executed in two contexts: - On-demand: when MC_FrontierRequest is received within EXPLORE, FGM recomputes the frontier set (if stale) and selects a target. - Incremental maintenance: subscribed to grid-map classification-change notifications (CMP--3) and updates cached frontier set whenever a cell state changes from UNKNOWN to FREE or UNKNOWN to BLOCKED, to allow O(1) retrieval during MC_FrontierRequest. All operations occur inside the periodic control loop.
Component.Timing: - Frontier recomputation worst-case: scans 4x8 grid (32 cells) each time; negligible vs control loop period. - Target selection heuristic loops over at most N_frontiers <= 32 candidates. - Triggered at most once per control loop for target selection; frontier set maintenance can occur multiple times per loop, but in practice limited by sensor update rate and smaller number of cell changes.
Component.Invariants: - Frontier set contains only UNKNOWN cells. - Each frontier cell has at least one 4-neighbor classified FREE. - Selected target always belongs to current frontier set and satisfies remaining-time constraint.
Component.Failures: - No frontier cells available: Trigger: FGM_FrontierSet empty. Effect: FGM_SelectedTarget indicates no target; MC interprets this as exploration completion candidate. Exploration-status module will eventually publish EXPLORATION_COMPLETED based on map completeness. - Time-limit constraint violation: Trigger: All frontier candidates have estimated travel time + mapping overhead > RemainingTimeMs. Effect: FGM_SelectedTarget reports no feasible targets; MC may continue short movements or immediately rely on exploration timeout event to transition to RETURN_TO_START.

Component.Name: Grid Map Module
Component.Purpose: Maintains the in-memory 4x8 grid map and associated wall segments, providing single-threaded access and update mechanisms: - Stores cell classification (UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED) and wall segment classification (UNKNOWN, PRESENT, ABSENT). - Initializes map and start cell. - Provides query and update operations. - Emits classification-change notifications. - Provides map completeness boolean for exploration logic. - Enforces immutability rules for classifications within a mission. - Applies free cell confirmation and blocked cell inference based on traversal completion and wall observations.

Signal.Name: GM_MapData
Signal.Description: Map structure exposed to planning and exploration modules via interface functions: - GetCellState(row, col). - GetWallState(edgeIndex / (row, col, orientation)). - GetNeighbors(row, col) considering map boundaries.

Signal.Name: GM_ClassificationChangeEvent
Signal.Description: Notification event issued when a cell or wall segment changes classification. Contains: - Type: {CELL, WALL}. - Indices. - PreviousState and NewState. Consumed by frontier-generation, replanning, and other modules.

Signal.Name: MM_MapUpdateRequest
Signal.Description: Update requests from mapping/wall detection module: - Update wall segment classifications (PRESENT/ABSENT) on appropriate boundaries. - Update cell classifications to FREE or BLOCKED. Each request includes: - Target indices. - Requested new classification. - Reason (e.g., "cell traversed without collision", "wall PRESENT between current and neighbor").

Signal.Name: CellCenterEntryEvent
Signal.Description: Event from Pose Estimation Module indicating that the robot has crossed from one cell center into an adjacent cell center. Contains: - fromCell (row,col), - toCell (row,col), - entryFace (direction of motion). Used for free cell confirmation on the traversed-to cell.

Signal.Name: CollisionAndConsistencyInfo
Signal.Description: Summary input from Motion Control and Reliability modules indicating: - Whether any collision or emergency stop occurred on the entry face during the transition. - Whether sensor consistency checks report contradictory evidence for the traversed-to cell.

Signal.Name: WallObservation
Signal.Description: From Mapping and Wall Detection: observation that a wall segment between current cell and a neighbor is classified PRESENT (or ABSENT). Contains: - currentCell. - neighborCell. - wallOrientation. - wallState (PRESENT or ABSENT).

Signal.Name: GM_MapCompletenessFlag
Signal.Description: Boolean flag indicating whether map is complete: - true: no cell or inter-cell wall segment remains UNKNOWN. - false: otherwise. Sampled by exploration-status module and mission control to decide exploration completion.

Signal.Name: Init_Request
Signal.Description: Initialization trigger received at system startup to: - Initialize all cells and wall segments to UNKNOWN. - Designate the fixed start cell and set it to FREE.

Component.Execution: - Map initialization at startup: 1) Allocate cell array [4][8], initialize each cell state to UNKNOWN. 2) Allocate wall arrays for vertical and horizontal walls as per inter-cell wall indexing. 3) Mark designated start cell (e.g., (0,0)) as FREE and record start coordinate for return-to-start and second-run behaviors. - During each control loop: 1) Free cell confirmation (LL--MAP--1.4.3): - On CellCenterEntryEvent from Pose Estimation: * Identify toCell (row,col) and entryFace. * If cell state of toCell is UNKNOWN and: + MotionControlModule reports that the transition into toCell completed without collision or emergency stop on the entry face, and + Reliability/consistency module indicates no contradictory evidence accumulated for that cell (i.e., sensor consistency checks do not show an established blocking wall or other conflict), * Then perform cell state transition UNKNOWN->FREE for toCell and emit GM_ClassificationChangeEvent for that cell. 2) Blocked cell inference (LL--MAP--1.4.4): - On WallObservation with wallState == PRESENT between currentCell and neighborCell: * Update corresponding wall segment from UNKNOWN to PRESENT (if currently UNKNOWN) and emit GM_ClassificationChangeEvent for that wall. * If neighborCell state is UNKNOWN: - Infer neighborCell is BLOCKED due to PRESENT wall separating it from current cell. - Set cell state UNKNOWN->BLOCKED for neighborCell and emit GM_ClassificationChangeEvent for that cell. * If neighborCell already FREE/BLOCKED, leave classification unchanged (respect immutability). 3) Wall ABSENT updates: - On WallObservation with wallState == ABSENT between two adjacent cells: * If wall segment is UNKNOWN, set UNKNOWN->ABSENT and emit classification change event. * If PRESENT/ABSENT already set, do not change (immutability). 4) Apply any explicit MM_MapUpdateRequest messages (e.g., additional FREE/ BLOCKED inferences) while enforcing allowed transitions: * Cell: UNKNOWN -> FREE or UNKNOWN -> BLOCKED only. * Wall: UNKNOWN -> PRESENT or UNKNOWN -> ABSENT only. 5) Trigger classification-change notifications for any changed entries. 6) Maintain counters of cells and walls with state UNKNOWN; update GM_MapCompletenessFlag to true when both counters reach zero.
Component.Timing: - All map operations are performed inside the control loop context (single-threaded), as required by LL--MAP--1.3.7. - Worst-case map operations (scanning 32 cells and associated walls) are bounded and fast relative to T. - Completeness flag may be recomputed incrementally using maintained counts to ensure O(1) behavior per update.
Component.Invariants: - Every cell state ∈ {UNKNOWN, FREE, BLOCKED}. - Every wall segment state ∈ {UNKNOWN, PRESENT, ABSENT}. - Cell state transitions: * UNKNOWN -> FREE (on successful traversal into cell with no collision and no contradictory evidence). * UNKNOWN -> BLOCKED (on inference from PRESENT wall between current and neighbor). * No transition from FREE/BLOCKED back to UNKNOWN or between FREE and BLOCKED within a mission. - Wall state transitions: * UNKNOWN -> PRESENT or UNKNOWN -> ABSENT based on wall observations. * No transition from PRESENT to ABSENT or vice versa, and no revert to UNKNOWN, within a mission. - Free/Blocked cell transitions are driven by: * MotionControlModule execution completion and CollisionAndConsistencyInfo for FREE. * WallDetection/Mapping’s WallObservation for BLOCKED.
Component.Failures: - Invalid classification transition: Trigger: request to change from FREE/BLOCKED to another value or PRESENT/ABSENT to another value. Handling: reject update and log diagnostic; preserve existing classification. - Out-of-range indices: Trigger: requests referencing cells or walls outside 4x8 map. Handling: discard update, log error.

Component.Name: Path Planner
Component.Purpose: Implements cell-based path planning on the 4x8 grid map: - Provides Dijkstra or A* search to compute paths between cells and goal regions. - Supports configurable traversal cost parameters for exploration and second run. - Handles multiple-goal planning for 2x2 goal regions. - Supports replanning on map updates that invalidate current paths. - Reports planning failures to mission control.

Signal.Name: PL_PathPlanningResponse
Signal.Description: Planning result for a given request: - RequestID. - Status: SUCCESS or specific failure. - Path: ordered list of cells from start to selected goal. - TotalCost and optionally EstimatedTimeMs (TotalCost transformed via cost-to-time mapping).

Signal.Name: PL_PlanningFailureEvent
Signal.Description: Simplified event to indicate no path exists given current map. Used by mission control to trigger alternative behaviors.

Signal.Name: MC_PathPlanningRequest
Signal.Description: Planning request specifying: - Mode {EXPLORE, RETURN_TO_START, SECOND_RUN}. - Start cell and goal specification (single cell or list of goal cells for goal region). - Traversal cost parameters ID. - Optional limit on max allowed cost.

Signal.Name: PL_MapReadAccess
Signal.Description: Access to grid map functions: - Query cell and wall states to build navigation graph. - Implement 4-connected neighbors only between FREE cells not separated by PRESENT walls.

Signal.Name: GM_ClassificationChangeEvent
Signal.Description: Planning invalidation hook: - When map changes mark cells from UNKNOWN to BLOCKED or walls UNKNOWN->PRESENT that intersect current path, planner flags current path as invalid. - Planner notifies mission control or motion control about invalidation.

Signal.Name: PL_GoalRegionCostInfo
Signal.Description: Intermediate data shared with goal-region selection: - For each 2x2 FREE candidate region, path cost from start to each cell.

Signal.Name: GR_GoalCells
Signal.Description: For second run, planner receives the selected goal region cells from goal-region selection module.

Component.Execution: - On each planning request: 1) Build search graph implicitly using grid map cell states and wall states. 2) Apply Dijkstra or A* depending on configuration: - Node = FREE cell. - Edge exists for orthogonally adjacent FREE cells if there is no PRESENT wall between them. - Edge cost determined from traversal cost model (straight and turn penalties). 3) For goal regions, treat each FREE goal cell as a possible terminal node; run multi-goal search or run algorithm once until all goals discovered. 4) On success, reconstruct path and compute total cost. 5) Map cost to time estimate using nominal cell traversal duration for derived heuristics. 6) Return PL_PathPlanningResponse. - Replanning: - When GM_ClassificationChangeEvent arrives and current path intersects newly BLOCKED or wall PRESENT segments, invalidate current path and notify mission control.
Component.Timing: - Path planning executed within control loop but may be limited to one planning run per loop. - With 32 cells, worst-case Dijkstra/A* is small; implementation must ensure worst-case search finishes within T, or planning can span multiple loops with incremental expansion. - Replanning triggered only when path invalidation occurs to avoid unnecessary computations.
Component.Invariants: - No path includes BLOCKED cells or crosses PRESENT walls. - Edge costs are non-negative. - Returned path cost is minimal given the traversal cost model.
Component.Failures: - No path found: Trigger: search exhausts all reachable cells without reaching any goal. Effect: PL_PathPlanningResponse with Status=FAILURE_NO_PATH; mission control handles this (e.g., abort or alternative behavior). - Map inconsistent during planning: Trigger: map changes while planning in progress. Handling: either lock snapshot of map per planning run or restart planning with new map; only consistent snapshots used for final paths.

Component.Name: Goal Region Identification and Selection
Component.Purpose: Determining and selecting the 2x2 goal region after exploration: - Identifies all candidate 2x2 regions composed entirely of FREE cells and reachable from start. - Requests path costs to each candidate from path planner. - Selects region with minimal optimal path cost. - Exposes chosen region to second-run planning and mission control.

Signal.Name: GR_CandidateRegions
Signal.Description: List of candidate 2x2 FREE regions: - Each candidate: 4 cell indices (row, col) representing the region. - Mark if reachable from start (using info from path planner or BFS).

Signal.Name: GM_MapSnapshot
Signal.Description: Read-only snapshot of FREE/BLOCKED cell states to enumerate all 2x2 blocks and check classification.

Signal.Name: GR_GoalRegionSelection
Signal.Description: Selected goal region: - Set of up to four FREE goal cells. - Region ID and path cost. Provided to planner for second-run fastest path computation.

Signal.Name: PL_GoalRegionCostInfo
Signal.Description: For each candidate region, cost of cheapest path from start cell to any of its 4 cells, using the traversal cost metric.

Signal.Name: GR_SelectedGoalRegionInfo
Signal.Description: Goal region exported to mission control for use in SECOND_RUN: - Region cells. - Chosen target goal cell (may be the one with minimum path cost).

Signal.Name: ExplorationCompletedTrigger
Signal.Description: Event from exploration-status/mission control indicating exploration is complete and goal region identification should execute exactly once.

Component.Execution: - On exploration completion: 1) Obtain map snapshot from grid map. 2) Enumerate all possible 2x2 blocks; for each, check all four cells are FREE. 3) For each all-FREE block, request path costs via path planner from start to each of the four cells. 4) Filter out non-reachable candidates (no path). 5) Select candidate whose minimal path cost is smallest. 6) Store and publish selected region.
Component.Timing: - Executed once after map completion detection. - With at most (3*7)=21 possible 2x2 blocks and 32 cells, enumeration and cost queries are trivial; must fit within several control loop cycles.
Component.Invariants: - Selected goal region, if any, is reachable from start. - Selected region cost is minimal among reachable candidate regions based on traversal cost metric.
Component.Failures: - No 2x2 FREE reachable region: Trigger: enumeration yields no candidate that is all FREE and reachable. Effect: report failure condition to mission control; second run may be disabled or alternative criteria used (e.g., single goal cell).

Component.Name: Mapping and Wall Detection Module
Component.Purpose: Uses processed sensor data and pose estimation to update grid map cells and walls: - Interprets ultrasonic and infrared readings to detect front and side walls with uncertainty and hysteresis. - Executes wall proximity thresholding with hysteresis on ultrasonic data. - Generates collision avoidance triggers when trajectory intersects imminent walls. - Applies free cell confirmation and blocked cell inference rules by generating appropriate update requests. - Issues map update requests to grid map.

Signal.Name: MM_MapUpdateRequest
Signal.Description: Requests to grid map: - Update wall segment classifications (PRESENT/ABSENT) on appropriate boundaries. - Update cell classifications to FREE or BLOCKED. These requests are limited to UNKNOWN->FREE/BLOCKED for cells and UNKNOWN->PRESENT/ABSENT for walls; they never attempt to override established non-UNKNOWN classifications.

Signal.Name: GM_CellAndWallStates
Signal.Description: Current classification around robot: - State of current cell, neighboring cells, and relevant walls. Used for consistent updates and blocked cell inference.

Signal.Name: MM_WallDetectionResult
Signal.Description: Interpreted wall detections relative to current cell: - Flags for front, left, right wall presence. - Estimated wall distances (with uncertainty intervals) in maze coordinates. Used by motion control for avoidance and by pose correction.

Signal.Name: MM_CollisionRiskEvent
Signal.Description: Event raised if predicted trajectory intersects a wall within one cell length, triggering avoidance maneuvers in motion control.

Signal.Name: SAL_FilteredSensorData
Signal.Description: Filtered sensor data from sensor abstraction and filtering: - Ultrasonic: (distance_cm, timestamp). - IR: left_flag, right_flag, timestamp. All invalid or out-of-range readings already sanitized.

Signal.Name: PoseEstimate
Signal.Description: Discrete pose estimate with cell-centered position and heading, plus flag indicating: - AtCellCenter or InTransitionBetweenCells. Used to decide when to update particular walls and cells.

Component.Execution: Per control loop: 1) Read filtered, validated sensor measurements and current pose. 2) If pose aligned to maze axes within tolerance and at/near cell center: - Use ultrasonic reading to check for front wall: * Compute measurement interval [d-12, d+12] cm. * Compute expected robot-center-to-wall distance interval (based on cell size 17.2 cm and robot radius 5.5 cm, plus wall thickness). * If intervals intersect and past hysteresis thresholds confirm stable detection, generate MM_MapUpdateRequest to set the corresponding front wall PRESENT (if currently UNKNOWN). - Use IR readings: * If left_flag or right_flag active and pose heading within 10° tolerance, infer side wall presence: - Account for 25° sensor mounting angle and 4 cm effective range and project to wall location on left/right boundary of current cell. - Generate MM_MapUpdateRequest to set corresponding lateral walls PRESENT (if currently UNKNOWN). 3) If robot transitions between cell centers without collisions: - Based on PoseEstimate and Motion Control feedback, generate MM_MapUpdateRequest to confirm FREE cells using the rule: * When transition into a new cell is successfully completed with no detected collision on entry face, and Reliability/consistency checks report no contradictory evidence for that cell, request UNKNOWN->FREE transition for that cell. 4) Apply wall proximity thresholding: - Use configurable upper/lower thresholds on filtered ultrasonic distance to determine wall vs free. - Maintain hysteresis state per direction to avoid flip-flopping. 5) Blocked cell inference: - For any wall detected PRESENT between current and neighbor cell (via detection logic in step 2), generate MM_MapUpdateRequest to: * Set wall UNKNOWN->PRESENT (if still UNKNOWN). * Infer neighbor cell BLOCKED if it is UNKNOWN, by requesting UNKNOWN->BLOCKED in GridMapModule. 6) Issue map update requests accordingly. 7) Consistency handling: - For cells and walls with state UNKNOWN: * Use N consecutive consistent readings (from ReliabilityModule) before issuing an MM_MapUpdateRequest to classify them. - For cells or walls already classified (FREE/BLOCKED or PRESENT/ABSENT): * Any contradictory sensor observation is treated as sensor error only; MM_MapUpdateRequest is not issued for changes that would violate immutability.
Component.Timing: - Invoked once per control loop after sensor filtering, before grid-map updates are applied. - Computations are simple algebra and local reasoning around at most a few walls; must complete within T.
Component.Invariants: - Free cell confirmation only after successful traversal without collision and no conflicting evidence requiring N consecutive contradictions. - Blocked cell inference always consistent with wall segment state PRESENT. - For map entries already classified (non-UNKNOWN), mapping never requests a new classification that would change FREE↔BLOCKED or PRESENT↔ABSENT.
Component.Failures: - Conflicting sensor data vs map: Trigger: new sensor readings indicate a wall where map indicates FREE or ABSENT, or free space where map indicates PRESENT, but map entries are no longer UNKNOWN. Handling: flagged as potential sensor error; map left unchanged; N consecutive reading counters may be tracked only for UNKNOWN entries; immutability preserved. - Pose misalignment > tolerance: Trigger: robot heading deviates >10° from maze axes. Effect: IR-based side wall inference suppressed until pose is corrected.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--MAP--1.2.1 LL--MAP--1.2.2 LL--MAP--1.2.3 LL--MAP--1.2.4 LL--MAP--1.3.2 LL--MAP--1.4.3 LL--MAP--1.4.4 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.2 LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.3.1 LL--INTERF--3.4.1 LL--ENV--1.2.1 LL--ENV--1.3.1 LL--HARDWARE--1.1.1

Component.Name: Sensor Abstraction and Filtering Layer
Component.Purpose: Wraps raw sensor APIs and provides normalized, filtered sensor data: - Calls ultrasonic_get_distance and IR status functions at control loop frequency. - Detects and handles invalid/out-of-range sensor readings. - Applies temporal filtering (moving average/median) to reduce noise. - Provides unified ultrasonic and IR measurement structures to mapping and reliability modules.

Signal.Name: SAL_SampledSensorData
Signal.Description: Raw per-cycle data from sensors: - Ultrasonic distance (float cm). - IR left/right flags (uint8_t to boolean). - Timestamps. Provided immediately after acquisition to filtering logic.

Signal.Name: Sched_ControlLoopTick
Signal.Description: Tick from scheduler at frequency matching configured sensor sampling rate. Used to ensure one sampling per iteration and enforce jitter constraints.

Signal.Name: SAL_FilteredSensorData
Signal.Description: Normalized and filtered sensor data: - Ultrasonic: (filteredDistance_cm, timestamp). - IR: (filteredLeftFlag, filteredRightFlag, timestamp). Filtering uses configured sampling rate (10–50 Hz, default 20 Hz) and temporal filtering method.

Signal.Name: SensorAvailabilityStatus
Signal.Description: Boolean flag indicating that at least one valid (non-invalid) ultrasonic distance and both IR readings have been successfully acquired at least once since mission start. Used by mission control to determine exploration start condition.

Signal.Name: RawUltrasonicApi
Signal.Description: Interface to ultrasonic_get_distance(), returning raw float distance or specific error/saturation codes. SAL is responsible for interpreting invalid codes.

Signal.Name: RawIrApi
Signal.Description: Interfaces to infrared_get_dsl_status() and infrared_get_dsr_status(). Returns uint8_t for each sensor interpreted as binary detection flags.

Component.Execution: Per control loop: 1) On Sched_ControlLoopTick, call ultrasonic_get_distance(), infrared_get_dsl_status(), infrared_get_dsr_status(). 2) Check for invalid values (negative distances, NaN, saturation codes): - Replace invalid ultrasonic values with "no obstacle" default (e.g., beyond maze size plus uncertainty) and mark as invalid for mapping decisions. - IR invalid values treated as no obstacle or flagged per configuration. 3) Push readings into fixed-length buffers (size determined by filter window) and compute filtered values (moving average or median). 4) Compose normalized structures with filtered values and current timestamp. 5) Update SensorAvailabilityStatus if all required sensors have delivered at least one valid reading.
Component.Timing: - Sampling exactly once per control loop iteration, at frequency in [10, 50] Hz. - Timing jitter limited by scheduler; SAL must not introduce additional variability. - Filtering operations O(windowSize) with small window sizes (e.g., 3–5 samples).
Component.Invariants: - SAL never forwards raw invalid values to mapping or planning modules. - Filtered data is always defined (either from valid readings or safe defaults).
Component.Failures: - Persistent invalid ultrasonic reading: Handling: maintain default "no obstacle" reading while flagging sensor degradation; mapping may rely more heavily on IR or map data. - Loss of IR data: Handling: treat IR as inactive (no obstacle) but rely on ultrasonic and map to avoid collisions; log error.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--1.1.1 LL--INTERF--1.1.2 LL--INTERF--2.1.1 LL--INTERF--3.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 LL--MAP--1.1.2

Component.Name: Motion Control and Collision Avoidance
Component.Purpose: Converts high-level path and motion primitives into motor_set commands with safety: - Executes cell-center motion primitives based on differential drive kinematics and cell geometry. - Enforces speed limitations, rate limiting, and emergency stop behavior. - Performs wall avoidance maneuvers when mapping reports imminent collisions. - Supports different speed profiles for exploration, return-to-start, and second run.

Signal.Name: MC_PathExecutionStatus
Signal.Description: Status of path execution: - For return-to-start: RUNNING, REACHED_START, PATH_INVALID, COLLISION_ABORTED. - For second run: RUNNING, SUCCESS_REACHED_GOAL, COLLISION_ABORTED.

Signal.Name: MC_MotionCommand
Signal.Description: High-level motion instruction from mission control including: - Path (sequence of cells) or next cell target. - Speed profile. - Safety constraints.

Signal.Name: EM_StopCommand
Signal.Description: Conceptual signal representing invocation of motor_set(0,0) when emergency conditions occur. After issuance, Motion Control enters emergency_stopped internal state.

Signal.Name: SAL_FilteredSensorData
Signal.Description: Used to monitor distance to obstacles for emergency-stop decisions based on configured emergency threshold and stopping distance.

Signal.Name: Motion_MotorCommand
Signal.Description: Invocation of motor_set(left_speed, right_speed) with speeds in [0,100]. Enforced to be at most once per control loop, respecting rate limiting on speed increments.

Signal.Name: Sched_ControlLoopTick
Signal.Description: Ensures motor commands are synchronized with control loop period T and updates internal timers for motion primitive durations.

Signal.Name: Motion_AvoidanceStatus
Signal.Description: Notifies mapping/mission control when avoidance maneuvers are in progress or completed.

Signal.Name: MM_WallDetectionResult
Signal.Description: Used to predict trajectory-wall intersections within one cell length and trigger avoidance maneuvers before collision.

Signal.Name: MM_CollisionRiskEvent
Signal.Description: Direct event indicating imminent collision within one cell length; triggers avoidance or emergency stop.

Component.Execution:
Internal modes: - NORMAL: executing commanded motion primitives. - AVOIDANCE: executing a short avoidance maneuver derived from wall detections. - EMERGENCY_STOPPED: motors at 0 after emergency stop; no movement resumed until new safe motion command accepted.
Per control loop: 1) Read current MC_MotionCommand (if any new), SAL_FilteredSensorData, and MM_WallDetectionResult. - If in EMERGENCY_STOPPED and no new MC_MotionCommand received that has been deemed safe by mission control (e.g., new planned trajectory): * Do not advance any primitive and keep motor_set(0,0); remain in EMERGENCY_STOPPED. 2) Emergency-stop check (only when not already in EMERGENCY_STOPPED): - If any sensor indicates obstacle closer than emergency threshold < nominal stopping distance (based on current speed and uncertainty): * Immediately call motor_set(0,0). * Set internal state to EMERGENCY_STOPPED. * Do not resume movement until a new safe MC_MotionCommand is received from Mission Control and explicitly accepted (resetting state to NORMAL). * Report path execution aborted and raise EmergencyStopEvent. 3) Else if MM_CollisionRiskEvent raised (and not in EMERGENCY_STOPPED): - Plan short avoidance maneuver (e.g., stop + in-place turn, or arc turn) such that: * Robot footprint plus clearance M does not intersect any wall. * Maneuver maintains or restores progress towards mission goal. - Transition to AVOIDANCE mode to execute this maneuver. 4) If executing a cell-center motion primitive in NORMAL or AVOIDANCE: - Use differential drive kinematics with control-loop period to incrementally move between cell centers or rotate to discrete headings. - Enforce speed limits: * ExplorationSpeed <= MaxSafeSpeedExploration. * SecondRunSpeed <= MaxSafeSpeedSecondRun < hardware limit and derived from safety constraints. 5) Before calling motor_set: - Apply rate limiting to speed increments between consecutive cycles, unless in the specific emergency-stop action where immediate reduction to 0 is allowed by safety requirement. 6) Issue one motor_set per cycle with computed speeds (or (0,0) in EMERGENCY_STOPPED).
Component.Timing: - Called once per control loop after mission control and path planning decisions. - Kinematic integration and collision checks use current T or measured cycle duration. - Emergency stop and avoidance decisions must be computed within one loop so that robot can stop within safe distance given sampling rate and uncertainties.
Component.Invariants: - Rate of change of motor_set speeds per control cycle does not exceed configured maximum per wheel, except in the special case of emergency stop where immediate deceleration to 0 is required. - Commanded speeds never exceed configured safety maximum computed from sampling rate, emergency distance threshold, and ultrasonic uncertainty. - Following an EmergencyStopEvent (transition to EMERGENCY_STOPPED), motor speeds remain at 0 and no motion primitives are advanced until a new MC_MotionCommand representing a safe trajectory is received and accepted, at which point internal state leaves EMERGENCY_STOPPED.
Component.Failures: - Emergency stop: Trigger: obstacle detected within emergency distance. Effect: immediate motor_set(0,0); path execution aborted; MC_PathExecutionStatus updated accordingly; internal state EMERGENCY_STOPPED persists until new safe command. - Inability to find avoidance maneuver: Trigger: all nearby configurations violate minimum clearance M. Effect: stay stopped, report failure and rely on higher-level replanning or manual recovery.
Component.DerivedFrom: LL--MAP--1.2.4 LL--RUN--1.1.2 LL--RUN--1.2.3 LL--INTERF--1.2.1 LL--INTERF--1.2.2 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.1 LL--HARDWARE--2.1.2 LL--HARDWARE--2.3.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2

Component.Name: Pose Estimation Module
Component.Purpose: Maintains discrete cell-level pose estimate: - Integrates differential drive kinematics over control loop periods. - Estimates current cell index (row, col) and discrete heading aligned with maze axes. - Corrects accumulated errors using detected walls and alignment. - Detects transitions between cell centers and notifies mapping module.

Signal.Name: PoseEstimate
Signal.Description: Current pose estimate including: - Cell indices (row, col). - Discrete heading index ∈ {NORTH, EAST, SOUTH, WEST}. - Continuous pose (x,y,θ) in maze frame if needed.

Signal.Name: CellCenterEntryEvent
Signal.Description: Event fired when pose crosses from one cell center to an adjacent cell center. Contains new cell index and direction of motion.

Signal.Name: Sched_ControlLoopTick
Signal.Description: Provides duration T or measured cycle duration used for integration of motor commands.

Signal.Name: PoseForMotionControl
Signal.Description: Detail of continuous pose and heading used by motion control to determine progress along primitives.

Signal.Name: MotionCommandedSpeeds
Signal.Description: Last-issued motor_set speeds for left and right wheels; used to integrate motion via kinematics.

Signal.Name: PoseAlignedFlag
Signal.Description: Flag to mapping indicating when pose has been snapped to maze axes and is reliable for wall mapping decisions.

Signal.Name: MM_WallDetectionResult
Signal.Description: Used to correct pose when walls indicate proximity to known cell boundaries; align orientation and position to nearest consistent cell center.

Component.Execution: Per control loop: 1) Use last cycle's motor speed commands and loop duration T to integrate differential drive kinematics: - Update continuous pose (x,y,θ). 2) Map continuous pose to discrete cell indices using grid coordinate system and cell dimension 17.2 cm. 3) If near theoretical cell center and heading near axis-aligned directions: - Snap heading to nearest axis orientation. - Snap position to nearest cell center consistent with wall locations. 4) Detect cell center transitions by comparing previous and current discrete cell indices. - Emit CellCenterEntryEvent when crossing occurs. 5) Provide PoseEstimate to consumers.
Component.Timing: - Updates once per control loop after motion command is known. - Needs to complete before mapping and mission control use updated pose.
Component.Invariants: - Discrete heading always one of four axis-aligned states. - PoseEstimate always maps to a valid cell within [0..3][0..7].
Component.Failures: - Large discrepancy between pose and walls: Handling: if no consistent cell center matches wall observations, keep last valid discrete pose and flag degraded pose quality for mapping.

Component.Name: Scheduler and Initialization
Component.Purpose: Provides main control loop timing and initialization signaling: - Implements periodic control loop at configurable period T. - Coordinates execution order: sensor sampling, map update, mission control, planning, motion control. - After init, raises INIT_COMPLETED indication. - Ensures sensor sampling rate equals control loop frequency.

Signal.Name: Sched_ControlLoopTick
Signal.Description: Clock tick for each control loop iteration: - Used by SAL, motion control, pose estimation to advance state.

Signal.Name: SAL_Config
Signal.Description: Configuration of sampling rate within [10, 50] Hz, default 20 Hz, used to determine T.

Signal.Name: InitCompletedFlag
Signal.Description: Binary flag/event set after all hardware and software initialization routines have been executed successfully. Mission control reads this to leave IDLE state.

Signal.Name: HardwareInitStatus
Signal.Description: Results of hardware initialization routines.

Component.Execution: - At startup: 1) Run hardware and software initialization routines. 2) Initialize grid map, pose, sensors, motion control configurations. 3) Set INIT_COMPLETED flag. - Periodic control loop (every T): 1) Emit Sched_ControlLoopTick. 2) Acquire sensor readings via SAL. 3) Update grid map via mapping and map module. 4) Update mission control state machine. 5) Trigger path planning for active state. 6) Execute motion control (issuing motor_set once per loop).
Component.Timing: - Control loop frequency equals configured sampling rate. - Jitter bounds derived from LL--RELIAB--1.2.1 must be met; scheduler ensures stable T.
Component.Invariants: - Exactly one pass through the ordered steps per control loop iteration. - No concurrent execution of map access functions (single-threaded model).
Component.Failures: - Failure in initialization step: Effect: INIT_COMPLETED not set; mission control remains in IDLE, robot not moving. - Timer overrun: If a loop iteration exceeds T, scheduler records overrun and may extend next period or skip non-critical tasks like logging.

Component.Name: Exploration Status and Time Limit Module
Component.Purpose: Monitors map completeness and exploration time to emit exploration status events: - Tracks elapsed exploration time. - Checks grid map completeness flag. - Emits EXPLORATION_COMPLETED or EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT events via system event queue. - Provides remaining exploration time to time-aware planning.

Signal.Name: ES_MapCompletenessQuery
Signal.Description: Query of boolean map complete flag each control loop or at configured intervals.

Signal.Name: GM_MapCompletenessFlag
Signal.Description: Boolean indicating whether map is complete.

Signal.Name: ExplorationEvents
Signal.Description: Queue entries created when: - Map completeness changes from false to true (EXPLORATION_COMPLETED). - Elapsed exploration time exceeds 5 minutes (EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT).

Signal.Name: RemainingExplorationTimeInfo
Signal.Description: ElapsedTimeMs and RemainingTimeMs (5 min - ElapsedTimeMs) available to mission control and exploration modules.

Signal.Name: MC_StateBroadcast
Signal.Description: Used to identify when EXPLORE starts/stops for time tracking (elapsed time measured only while in exploration phase or from exploration start as defined).

Signal.Name: TimeAwarePlanningInfo
Signal.Description: Remaining exploration time and nominal cell traversal duration parameter exposed for frontier target selection.

Signal.Name: NominalTraversalParam
Signal.Description: Configurable parameter (ms per cell) used to convert path steps into estimated traversal times.

Component.Execution: - On entering EXPLORE: - Record exploration start time (mission time). - Each control loop: 1) Compute elapsed exploration time from start time. 2) If elapsed >= 5 minutes: - Emit EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event exactly once. 3) If GM_MapCompletenessFlag is true and not yet signaled: - Emit EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event exactly once. 4) Update RemainingExplorationTimeInfo for other modules.
Component.Timing: - Event checks happen once per control loop. - Uses scheduler-provided mission time or tick counts to compute elapsed time accurately.
Component.Invariants: - At most one EXPLORATION_COMPLETED and one EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event per mission. - RemainingTimeMs never negative; clamped at zero.
Component.Failures: - Time base failure (no time information): Handling: treat as no remaining time; likely cause immediate timeout and safe return behavior.

State.Name: IDLE
State.StateAction: - Keep motors stopped (no MC_MotionCommand). - Monitor INIT_COMPLETED and SensorAvailabilityStatus flags. - Initialize exploration-related timers and flags upon future exit.

State.Name: EXPLORE
State.StateAction: - Periodically request frontier targets from CMP--2. - Send MC_PathPlanningRequest to CMP--4 for selected frontier cell. - If path available, issue MC_MotionCommand in exploration speed profile to CMP--8. - Monitor ExplorationEvents and pose to detect exploration completion/timeout.

State.Name: RETURN_TO_START
State.StateAction: - Invoke return-to-start service: request path from current cell to start cell. - Command motion control to execute return path. - Monitor ReturnToStartStatus for completion or failure.

State.Name: SECOND_RUN
State.StateAction: - Request fastest path from start cell to selected goal region with time-optimized traversal cost. - Command motion control to execute path with higher speed profile respecting safety. - Monitor second run execution status to derive success/failure.

State.Name: COMPLETED
State.StateAction: - Cease issuing new MC_MotionCommand (motors left stopped by motion control). - Maintain final second-run status in MC_SecondRunStatusOutput for external reporting.

Transition.Trigger: INIT_COMPLETED indication present AND SensorAvailabilityStatus is true, both sampled within control loop.
Transition.Guard: - No critical errors in initialization. - Map initialized with start cell set to FREE.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Reset exploration timers and elapsed time counters. - Initialize exploration modules (frontier generation, exploration-status).

Transition.Trigger: - Receipt of EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event OR - Receipt of EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT event from exploration-status module, whichever occurs first.
Transition.Guard: - Current motion commands are safe to stop or replan.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Stop issuing further frontier requests. - Issue return-to-start path planning request from current cell to start cell. - Set internal reason code (COMPLETED or TIMEOUT).

Transition.Trigger: - ReturnToStartStatus indicates REACHED_START. - Exploration previously declared complete (EXPLORATION_COMPLETED event had occurred; TIMEOUT alone may still allow second run if map sufficient).
Transition.Guard: - Robot pose at start cell (verified by pose estimate and possibly small tolerance). - Start cell is FREE in map.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Request goal region identification/selection if not already done. - Request fastest path plan (SECOND_RUN mode). - Initialize MC_SecondRunStatusOutput to RUNNING.

Transition.Trigger: ReturnToStartStatus indicates PLANNING_FAILURE or ABORTED_COLLISION and no alternative route available.
Transition.Guard: - Policy: mission ends if robot cannot return safely.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Set MC_SecondRunStatusOutput to FAILURE. - Ensure motion commands are stopped.

Transition.Trigger: - SecondRunExecutionStatus == SUCCESS_REACHED_GOAL OR - SecondRunExecutionStatus == FAILURE_COLLISION OR - Planning failure for fastest path.
Transition.Guard: None beyond trigger; any terminal status ends mission.
Transition.TransitionAction: - Latch MC_SecondRunStatusOutput to SUCCESS or FAILURE depending on reported status. - Stop issuing path execution commands to motion control.

Data.Content: Traversal Cost Model Parameters: - Define cost units such that: baseStraightCost = 1.0 per move between adjacent FREE cells with no turn. turnPenalty = configurable >= 0 for turns between edges (e.g., 0.5). - Exploration profile: - Emphasize information gain while maintaining reasonable path length; keep baseStraightCost small, turnPenalty modest. - Second-run profile: - Set parameters corresponding to estimated travel time: costPerStraightCell = cellLength / nominalSpeed. costPerTurn = extra time to decelerate, turn, accelerate. - Cost computation: For each path: TotalCost = sum(edgeCosts) where edgeCost = baseStraightCost (+turnPenalty when heading changes).

Time-Aware Planning and Nominal Cell Traversal Duration: - Configuration parameter: uint32_t nominalCellTraversalMs; // default derived from measured robot speed at exploration speed. - Remaining exploration time: remainingTimeMs = max(0, explorationTimeLimitMs - elapsedExplorationMs). - Frontier feasibility test: For candidate frontier at distance N_cells from current cell: estTravelMs = N_cells * nominalCellTraversalMs; estMappingOverheadMs = nominalCellTraversalMs; // simple fixed overhead. If estTravelMs + estMappingOverheadMs > remainingTimeMs, discard candidate.

Ultrasonic Uncertainty and Clearance Checks: - Ultrasonic measurement model: Each distance reading d_cm is expanded to [d_cm - 12, d_cm + 12] cm interval. - Robot model: Radius R = 5.5 cm. Wall thickness ~1.6 cm. Cell size L = 17.2 cm. - Expected center-to-wall distance for front wall when centered in cell: approxDistance = L/2 + wallThickness/2 ≈ 8.6 + 0.8 = 9.4 cm (approximate; derived precisely in implementation). - Clearance margin M: Configurable M > 0 cm, e.g., 2–5 cm; planned trajectories ensure robot center stays at least R + M away from any wall boundary. - Emergency stop threshold: Derived from maximum speed, stopping distance, and uncertainty interval; ensures robot can stop before wall even in worst-case measurement error.
Data.DerivedFrom: LL--INTERF--2.3.1 LL--HARDWARE--1.1.1 LL--ENV--1.2.1 LL--ENV--1.3.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.1 LL--RELIAB--1.1.2 LL--MAP--1.2.1

Data.Content: Sensor Filtering and Consistency Parameters: - Sampling frequency: configurable in [10, 50] Hz; default 20 Hz. controlLoopPeriod T = 1 / samplingFrequency. - Filter windows: ultrasonicMovingAverageWindow = 3–5 samples. IRmovingAverageOrDebounceWindow = 2–3 samples. - Consistency threshold N for determining first classification of UNKNOWN map entries: integer N ∈ [2, 10], default 3. Implementation: maintain counters per UNKNOWN cell/wall; only change classification from UNKNOWN to FREE/BLOCKED or PRESENT/ABSENT when N consecutive readings indicate the same state. After a map entry becomes FREE/BLOCKED or PRESENT/ABSENT, immutability applies and N is no longer used to attempt overrides; conflicting evidence is treated as sensor error only.

Motion Primitive Specification: - Straight move primitive: Move from one cell center to adjacent in direction of current discrete heading. Steps: 1) Compute distance to travel = cellLength (17.2 cm). 2) Compute required duration based on exploration or second-run speed (cm/s). 3) Execute repeated motor_set commands with equal left/right speeds for computed number of control loop cycles. - Turn in place primitive: Rotate by ±90° or 180° to align with maze axis. Use differential drive kinematics to determine wheel speeds and duration for desired angle based on robot geometry. - Arc turn primitive for avoidance: Use left/right speeds with appropriate ratio to follow circular arc of radius >= R + M to maintain clearance from walls.

Event Queue for Exploration and Mission Events: - Data structure: Circular buffer of fixed size (e.g., 16 entries) storing: struct Event { EventType type; uint32_t timestamp; EventPayload payload; }; - Supported event types include: EXPLORATION_COMPLETED, EXPLORATION_TIMEOUT, PATH_INVALIDATED, RETURN_COMPLETED, SECOND_RUN_COMPLETED. - Usage: - Modules (e.g., exploration-status) enqueue events. - Mission control dequeues and processes all available events each control loop.
